name: bottom-up-bond-scout
description: Randomly chooses an object from the workspace (weighted by intra-string salience), picks a neighbor, selects a bond facet, determines descriptors and bond category, and proposes a bond between them if compatible.
execute_body:
# Bottom-up bond scout: choose object, neighbor, propose bond
from server.engine.workspace_objects import Letter

obj = choose_object('intra')
if obj is None:
    fizzle()

neighbor = choose_neighbor(obj)
if neighbor is None:
    fizzle()

# Find bond facets both objects share
obj_facets = [d.description_type for d in obj.descriptions
              if any(lk.to_node.name == 'plato-bond-facet'
                     for lk in d.description_type.category_links)]
neighbor_facets = [d.description_type for d in neighbor.descriptions
                   if any(lk.to_node.name == 'plato-bond-facet'
                          for lk in d.description_type.category_links)]
common_facets = [f for f in obj_facets if f in neighbor_facets]
if not common_facets:
    fizzle()

# Choose a bond facet weighted by activation
facet_weights = [max(0.1, f.activation) for f in common_facets]
bond_facet = weighted_pick(common_facets, facet_weights)

# Get descriptors for this facet
obj_desc = None
neighbor_desc = None
for d in obj.descriptions:
    if d.description_type is bond_facet:
        obj_desc = d.descriptor
        break
for d in neighbor.descriptions:
    if d.description_type is bond_facet:
        neighbor_desc = d.descriptor
        break
if obj_desc is None or neighbor_desc is None:
    fizzle()

# Determine bond category
bond_cat = get_bond_category(obj_desc, neighbor_desc)
if bond_cat is None:
    fizzle()

# Determine direction
direction = None
sameness = get_node('plato-sameness')
if bond_cat is not sameness:
    if obj.left_string_pos < neighbor.left_string_pos:
        direction = get_node('plato-right')
    else:
        direction = get_node('plato-left')

propose_bond(obj, neighbor, bond_cat, bond_facet, obj_desc, neighbor_desc, direction)

name: top-down-bond-scout:category
description: Top-down scout driven by an active bond-category slipnet node. Chooses a string, picks an object and neighbor, then proposes a bond of the specified category if the descriptors match.

name: top-down-bond-scout:direction
description: Top-down scout driven by an active direction slipnet node. Chooses a string, picks an object, selects the neighbor in the specified direction, and proposes a directed bond.

name: bond-evaluator
description: Evaluates a proposed bond's strength and probabilistically accepts or rejects it.
execute_body:
# Bond evaluator
bond = args.get('structure')
if bond is None:
    fizzle()

if not evaluate_structure(bond):
    fizzle()

# Post a bond-builder
post_codelet('bond-builder', round(bond.strength), structure=bond)

name: bond-builder
description: Attempts to build an evaluated bond. Fights incompatible structures, breaks losers, builds the bond.

name: top-down-group-scout:category
description: Top-down scout driven by an active group-category slipnet node. Chooses a string, picks an object, scans adjacent bonds matching the group category, and proposes a group.
execute_body:
# Top-down group scout by category
# Scheme: groups.ss:418-486 — uses scan-bonds with count limit
from server.engine.groups import Group

grp_cat_node = args.get('slipnode')
if grp_cat_node is None:
    fizzle()

string = choose_string()
if not string.bonds:
    fizzle()

obj = choose_string_object(string, 'intra')
if obj is None:
    fizzle()

# Find the associated bond category
bond_cat = None
for lk in grp_cat_node.lateral_links:
    if lk.label_node and lk.label_node.name == 'plato-bond-category':
        bond_cat = lk.to_node
        break
if bond_cat is None:
    fizzle()

# Scan limit from probability distribution over [1..string_length]
# Scheme: workspace-strings.ss:56-59 — weighted by index^2
max_scan = rng.randint(string.length) + 1

# Scan right for matching bonds (count-limited per Scheme scan-bonds)
objects = [obj]
bonds = []
current = obj
for _scan in range(max_scan):
    b = current.right_bond
    if b is None or not b.is_built:
        break
    if b.bond_category is not bond_cat:
        break
    objects.append(b.right_object)
    bonds.append(b)
    current = b.right_object

if len(objects) < 2:
    fizzle()

direction = bonds[0].direction if bonds else None
bond_facet = bonds[0].bond_facet if bonds else get_node('plato-letter-category')

group = Group(string, grp_cat_node, bond_facet, direction, objects, bonds)
group.time_stamp = codelet_count
post_codelet('group-evaluator', round(grp_cat_node.activation), structure=group)

name: top-down-group-scout:direction
description: Top-down scout driven by an active direction slipnet node. Chooses a string, picks an object, scans bonds in the specified direction, and proposes a group.
execute_body:
# Top-down group scout by direction
# Scheme: groups.ss:489-544 — uses scan-bonds with count limit
from server.engine.groups import Group

dir_node = args.get('slipnode')
if dir_node is None:
    fizzle()

string = choose_string()
if not string.bonds:
    fizzle()

obj = choose_string_object(string, 'intra')
if obj is None:
    fizzle()

# Find initial bond in specified direction
initial_bond = None
if dir_node.name == 'plato-right' and obj.right_bond and obj.right_bond.is_built:
    if obj.right_bond.direction is dir_node:
        initial_bond = obj.right_bond
elif dir_node.name == 'plato-left' and obj.left_bond and obj.left_bond.is_built:
    if obj.left_bond.direction is dir_node:
        initial_bond = obj.left_bond

if initial_bond is None:
    fizzle()

bond_cat = initial_bond.bond_category
objects = [obj]
bonds = [initial_bond]
current = initial_bond.right_object if dir_node.name == 'plato-right' else initial_bond.left_object
objects.append(current)

# Count-limited scan (Scheme: scan-bonds with max-num-to-scan)
max_scan = rng.randint(string.length) + 1
for _scan in range(max_scan):
    next_bond = current.right_bond if dir_node.name == 'plato-right' else current.left_bond
    if next_bond is None or not next_bond.is_built:
        break
    if next_bond.bond_category is not bond_cat or next_bond.direction is not dir_node:
        break
    next_obj = next_bond.right_object if dir_node.name == 'plato-right' else next_bond.left_object
    objects.append(next_obj)
    bonds.append(next_bond)
    current = next_obj

if len(objects) < 2:
    fizzle()

# Find group category from bond category
grp_cat = None
for lk in bond_cat.lateral_links:
    if lk.label_node and lk.label_node.name == 'plato-group-category':
        grp_cat = lk.to_node
        break
if grp_cat is None:
    fizzle()

bond_facet = bonds[0].bond_facet
group = Group(string, grp_cat, bond_facet, dir_node, objects, bonds)
group.time_stamp = codelet_count
post_codelet('group-evaluator', round(dir_node.activation), structure=group)

name: group-scout:whole-string
description: Bottom-up scout that attempts to form a group spanning an entire string.
execute_body:
# Whole-string group scout
# Scheme: groups.ss:545-580 — walks bonds from leftmost to rightmost
from server.engine.groups import Group

string = choose_string()
if not string.bonds:
    fizzle()

# Check if bonds span the whole string
letters = string.letters
if len(letters) < 2:
    fizzle()

# Walk right bonds from leftmost letter (bounded by string length)
objects = [letters[0]]
bonds = []
current = letters[0]
for _scan in range(string.length):
    b = current.right_bond
    if b is None or not b.is_built:
        break
    objects.append(b.right_object)
    bonds.append(b)
    current = b.right_object
    if current is letters[-1]:
        break

if current is not letters[-1] or len(bonds) == 0:
    fizzle()

# Check all bonds have same category
first_cat = bonds[0].bond_category
if not all(b.bond_category is first_cat for b in bonds):
    fizzle()

direction = bonds[0].direction
bond_facet = bonds[0].bond_facet

# Find group category
grp_cat = None
for lk in first_cat.lateral_links:
    if lk.label_node and lk.label_node.name == 'plato-group-category':
        grp_cat = lk.to_node
        break
if grp_cat is None:
    fizzle()

group = Group(string, grp_cat, bond_facet, direction, objects, bonds)
group.time_stamp = codelet_count
post_codelet('group-evaluator', 35, structure=group)

name: group-evaluator
description: Evaluates a proposed group's strength. If accepted, posts a group-builder.
execute_body:
# Group evaluator
group = args.get('structure')
if group is None:
    fizzle()

if not evaluate_structure(group):
    fizzle()

post_codelet('group-builder', round(group.strength), structure=group)

name: group-builder
description: Attempts to build an evaluated group.

name: bottom-up-bridge-scout
description: Randomly selects a bridge type weighted by inverse mapping strength, chooses two objects, computes concept-mappings, and proposes a bridge.
execute_body:
# Bottom-up bridge scout
from server.engine.bridges import Bridge, BRIDGE_TOP, BRIDGE_BOTTOM, BRIDGE_VERTICAL
from server.engine.concept_mappings import ConceptMapping

# Choose bridge type weighted by inverse mapping strength
types_and_strings = [('top', BRIDGE_TOP, workspace.initial_string, workspace.modified_string)]
types_and_strings.append(('vertical', BRIDGE_VERTICAL, workspace.initial_string, workspace.target_string))
if justify_mode:
    types_and_strings.append(('bottom', BRIDGE_BOTTOM, workspace.target_string, workspace.answer_string))

weights = [max(0.1, 100 - mapping_strength(t[0])) for t in types_and_strings]
chosen = weighted_pick(types_and_strings, weights)
bt_name, bt, string1, string2 = chosen

if string2 is None:
    fizzle()

# Choose objects from each string weighted by inter-string salience
key = 'horizontal_inter' if bt != BRIDGE_VERTICAL else 'vertical_inter'
obj1 = choose_string_object(string1, key)
obj2 = choose_string_object(string2, key)
if obj1 is None or obj2 is None:
    fizzle()

# Compute concept mappings between descriptions
cms = []
for d1 in obj1.descriptions:
    for d2 in obj2.descriptions:
        if d1.description_type is d2.description_type:
            label = None
            if d1.descriptor is d2.descriptor:
                label = get_node('plato-identity')
            else:
                for lk in d1.descriptor.outgoing_links:
                    if lk.to_node is d2.descriptor and lk.label_node:
                        label = lk.label_node
                        break
            cm = ConceptMapping(d1.description_type, d1.descriptor, d2.description_type, d2.descriptor, label)
            cms.append(cm)

if not cms:
    fizzle()

bridge = Bridge(obj1, obj2, bt, cms)
bridge.time_stamp = codelet_count
avg_strength = sum(cm.strength() for cm in cms) / len(cms)
post_codelet('bridge-evaluator', round(avg_strength), structure=bridge)

name: important-object-bridge-scout
description: Selects a bridge type, chooses object1 by relative importance, finds a matching object2 via distinguishing descriptions.

name: bridge-evaluator
description: Evaluates a proposed bridge's strength and probabilistically accepts or rejects it.
execute_body:
# Bridge evaluator
bridge = args.get('structure')
if bridge is None:

if not evaluate_structure(bridge):
    fizzle()

post_codelet('bridge-builder', round(bridge.strength), structure=bridge)

name: bridge-builder
description: Builds an evaluated bridge into the workspace.

name: bottom-up-description-scout
description: Chooses an object, selects a relevant description, follows property links, and proposes a new description.
execute_body:
# Bottom-up description scout
obj = choose_object('intra')
if obj is None:
    fizzle()

# Choose a relevant description weighted by activation
relevant = [d for d in obj.descriptions if d.is_relevant()]
if not relevant:
    fizzle()

weights = [max(0.1, d.descriptor.activation) for d in relevant]
chosen = weighted_pick(relevant, weights)

# Follow property links from the descriptor
property_links = chosen.descriptor.property_links
if not property_links:
    fizzle()

# Pick a property link
link = rng.pick(property_links)
new_descriptor = link.to_node

# Find the description type for this new descriptor
new_desc_type = None
for lk in new_descriptor.category_links:
    new_desc_type = lk.to_node
    break
if new_desc_type is None:
    fizzle()

# Check if this description already exists
for d in obj.descriptions:
    if d.description_type is new_desc_type and d.descriptor is new_descriptor:
        fizzle()

propose_description(obj, new_desc_type, new_descriptor)

name: top-down-description-scout
description: Top-down scout driven by an active description-type slipnet node. Chooses an object and proposes a description of the specified type.
execute_body:
# Top-down description scout
desc_type_node = args.get('slipnode')
if desc_type_node is None:
    fizzle()

obj = choose_object('intra')
if obj is None:
    fizzle()

# Check if object already has this description type
for d in obj.descriptions:
    if d.description_type is desc_type_node:
        fizzle()

# Find possible descriptors for this type
possible = []
for inst_link in desc_type_node.instance_links:
    candidate = inst_link.to_node
    possible.append(candidate)

if not possible:
    fizzle()

# Choose a descriptor weighted by activation
weights = [max(0.1, d.activation) for d in possible]
descriptor = weighted_pick(possible, weights)

propose_description(obj, desc_type_node, descriptor)

name: description-evaluator
description: Evaluates a proposed description and probabilistically accepts or rejects it.

name: description-builder
description: Builds an evaluated description by attaching it to its object.

name: rule-scout
description: Discovers transformation rules by examining horizontal bridges.
execute_body:
# Rule scout
from server.engine.rules import Rule, RuleClause, RuleChange, RULE_TOP, CLAUSE_INTRINSIC

top_bridges = get_built_bridges('top')
if not top_bridges:
    fizzle()

# Build rule clauses from bridge concept mappings
clauses = []
for bridge in top_bridges:
    for cm in bridge.concept_mappings:
        if cm.is_slippage:
            change = RuleChange(
                dimension=cm.description_type1,
                from_descriptor=cm.descriptor1,
                to_descriptor=cm.descriptor2,
                relation=cm.label,
            )
            obj_desc = (cm.description_type1, cm.descriptor1)
            clause = RuleClause(
                clause_type=CLAUSE_INTRINSIC,
                object_description=obj_desc,
                changes=[change],
            )
            clauses.append(clause)

if not clauses:
    fizzle()

rule = Rule(RULE_TOP, clauses)
rule.time_stamp = codelet_count
rule.supporting_bridges = list(top_bridges)
rule.compute_quality(meta)
rule.transcribe_to_english()

post_codelet('rule-evaluator', 63, structure=rule)

name: rule-evaluator
description: Evaluates a proposed rule and probabilistically accepts or rejects it.
execute_body:
# Rule evaluator
rule = args.get('structure')
if rule is None:
    fizzle()

rule.compute_quality(meta)

if not evaluate_structure(rule):
    fizzle()

post_codelet('rule-builder', 63, structure=rule)

name: rule-builder
description: Builds a rule and posts an answer-finder.
execute_body:
# Rule builder
rule = args.get('structure')
if rule is None:
    fizzle()

build_structure(rule)

# Post answer-finder or answer-justifier
urgency = max(1, round(100 - temperature.value))
if justify_mode:
    post_codelet('answer-justifier', urgency)
else:
    post_codelet('answer-finder', urgency)

name: answer-finder
description: Attempts to produce an answer by translating a top rule across the vertical mapping.
execute_body:
# Answer finder
from server.engine.answers import compute_answer_quality
from server.engine.trace import SNAG, ANSWER_FOUND

# Check mapping strengths are sufficient
top_strength = mapping_strength('top')
vert_strength = mapping_strength('vertical')
if top_strength < 20 or vert_strength < 20:
    fizzle()

# Get supported top rules
supported = workspace.get_supported_rules(True)
if not supported:
    fizzle()

# Choose best rule
rule_weights = [max(0.1, r.quality) for r in supported]
chosen_rule = weighted_pick(supported, rule_weights)

# Translate the rule via vertical bridges
translated = translate_rule(chosen_rule)
if translated is None:
    # Snag: no vertical bridges to translate through
    record_event(SNAG, description='No vertical bridges for rule translation')
    fizzle()

# Apply the translated rule to the target string
answer_text = apply_rule(translated)
if answer_text is None:
    # Snag: rule application failed (e.g., successor of z)
    record_event(SNAG, description='Rule application failed (snag)')
    fizzle()

# Compute answer quality
quality = compute_answer_quality(chosen_rule.quality, temperature.value, meta)

# Report the answer
report_answer(answer_text, quality, top_rule=chosen_rule)

name: answer-justifier
description: Used in justify mode to validate a given answer.

name: thematic-bridge-scout
description: Self-watching codelet that scouts for bridges guided by active Themespace themes.

name: progress-watcher
description: Self-watching codelet that monitors system progress.

name: jootser
description: Self-watching codelet that detects and escapes repetitive failure patterns (Jumping Out Of The System).
execute_body:
# Jootser
from server.engine.jootsing import attempt_jootsing

if not self_watching:
    fizzle()

result = attempt_jootsing(trace, themespace, meta, commentary=commentary, codelet_count=codelet_count)

if result.give_up:
    # Give up: too many failed attempts
    pass
elif result.pattern_detected and result.negative_pattern:
    # Clamp negative theme pattern to inhibit stuck interpretation
    themespace.clamp_negative_pattern(result.negative_pattern)
    # Clamp bottom-up codelet pattern to force exploration
    pattern = meta.codelet_patterns.get('bottom-up-codelet-pattern', [])
    if pattern:
        coderack.clamp_pattern(pattern)
    trace.record_clamp_start(codelet_count, temperature.value)

name: breaker
description: Randomly breaks existing structures to allow restructuring. Probabilistically fizzles at low temperature.
execute_body:
# Breaker codelet
# Probabilistically fizzle at low temperature (prefer to break at high temp)
if not prob(temperature.value / 100.0):
    fizzle()

# Get all built non-rule structures
structures = [s for s in workspace.all_structures
              if hasattr(s, 'is_built') and s.is_built
              and not hasattr(s, 'is_top_rule')]
if not structures:
    fizzle()

# Choose a structure randomly
target = rng.pick(structures)

# Probabilistically break based on weakness (temperature-adjusted)
weakness = target.weakness() / 100.0
break_prob = temp_adjusted_prob(weakness)
if prob(break_prob):
    break_structure(target)
